scene: Scene 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: The identical 'Create solicitation' button appears on all eight Draft (not-ready) plan cards in the PLANNING grid, as well as on the finished R6 study card. This is a contradictory affordance: it implies you can launch a solicitation from an unfinished/unapproved plan, which undercuts the scene's concept that the call starts from a FINISHED plan. No dead end or error, so capped at 3 rather than 2.
fix_recommendation: Suppress or disable the 'Create solicitation' affordance on Draft (not-approved) plan cards so it only appears where the concept holds — on approved plans/studies. Alternative: keep it visible but disabled with a tooltip ('approve this plan to solicit'). Needs a product pick between hide-entirely / disable-with-tooltip / only-on-approved.

scene: Scene 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Up from iter4's 2: the empty 'Untitled study · 0 wards' placeholder card is gone and the finished R6 study card is clean. But it is held at 3 — the load-bearing finished 'R6 — Attakar × Gura' study card is small and sits at the very bottom under a dense 8-card Draft 'PLANNING' grid that dominates the viewport. Visual hierarchy fights the narrative: a viewer scanning top-down hits the muted Draft cards first and the finished study card last. Host chrome is grounding, not counted.
fix_recommendation: Make the finished R6 study card the focal point of the page: either move the 'Plan groups & studies' section above the PLANNING grid, or give the finished study a hero treatment (larger card, top placement) so the READY-TO-SOLICIT card leads rather than the draft inventory. Product hierarchy change — not a capture crop.

scene: Scene 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: No concrete user/role is named on screen (only the abstract 'LLO/funder'); the problem framing ('before any opportunity is live') lands but a true outsider must infer the actor. Per the deduction rule (no concrete user/role named), capped at 3.
fix_recommendation: Name the actor on the page — a 'Maya / Program owner' context label or a one-line intro ('recruiting a survey firm from your finished study') in the header — so a cold viewer gets the role without narration.

scene: Scene 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The real two-ward map (Attakar green / Gura blue polygons) renders correctly and the 'Coverage areas (from micro-plan)' panel with 'Snapshotted at creation' is present and legible. Held at 3 because, in the tall full-page create-form strip, the load-bearing coverage panel (the one thing this scene must prove) sits small and low under several mostly-empty form fields competing for the eye. Capture-strip caveat noted (do not deduct the product for the full-page-strip artifact itself), but the product would benefit from presenting the coverage panel more prominently in a normal viewport.
fix_recommendation: Present the rendered coverage map + '2 wards / 840 work areas' provenance prominently in the create form — a focused/expanded coverage section, or progressive disclosure that surfaces the attached-coverage proof above the long form body. This is a PRODUCT presentation change; do NOT 'fix' it with a camera zoom/crop/anchor or full_page:false.

scene: Scene 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The Award screen names the grantee (Amina Okafor) and the R6 survey and makes the award action clear, so the role/action half of the concept is present. But the load-bearing second half of the concept_claim — that provisioning a live Connect opportunity is the DEFERRED next step (not done today) — is entirely absent from the UI; it lives only in the narration. A one-sentence on-screen read gets 'award a firm', not 'award is shipped, live-opp is next'. Held at 3.
fix_recommendation: Surface the planning-vs-execution boundary in the award UI: a muted helper line ('Awarding records this firm; standing up their live Connect opportunity is a separate next step, not yet built'), or a small disabled 'Provision opportunity (coming soon)' chip. Needs a product pick between a helper line / disabled chip / a two-step indicator.

scene: Scene 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The amber/yellow warning callout renders its body line ('The grantee's organization ID and budget allocation will be recorded...') in low-contrast orange text on a pale-yellow background — a likely WCAG AA failure on a load-bearing explanatory line. Per the low-contrast deduction rule, capped at 3. Layout, type hierarchy, and control consistency are otherwise good.
fix_recommendation: Darken the callout body text (a darker amber / near-black) to meet WCAG AA contrast against the yellow background. One concrete CSS/token change.

scene: Scene 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The screen traces cleanly to S7's first clause (the shipped recruit-and-review→award step — it names the reviewed firm and records org ID + budget). But S7's second clause — turning an award into a live Connect opportunity is the intended next step, not yet built (Gap G1) — has zero on-screen provenance; the gap-honesty half is represented only in narration. The screen correctly does NOT claim to provision an opportunity (no contradiction), so capped at 3 rather than 2.
fix_recommendation: Carry S7's gap-honesty half into the artifact itself: a stepper that visually stops at 'Award' with the next 'Provision live opportunity' stage greyed/disabled, or a post-award success state that explicitly says provisioning is a separate not-yet-built step. Needs a product pick between stepper / disabled next-stage button / post-award note.
